description: A sustainability specialist at a large food processing plant in Phoenix, AZ needs to evaluate whether a solar linear Fresnel collector field could supply process steam for their pasteurization lines. Create a Python script at /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/linear_fresnel_iph.py using PySAM's LinearFresnelDsgIph module (DSGLIPHLCOHCalculator configuration) to provide a preliminary annual thermal energy estimate. The model must use a Phoenix, AZ weather file from SAM's solar_resource directory and be configured for an aperture area of approximately 10,000 m², a design outlet temperature of 250 °C, an inlet temperature of 150 °C, and an operating pressure of 25 bar (2500 kPa). Save the resulting metrics (annual_energy_kwh, capacity_factor_percent, solar_field_aperture_m2, design_outlet_temp_c, design_inlet_temp_c, operating_pressure_bar, latitude, longitude, and weather_file_used) to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/linear_fresnel_iph_results.json.
